NVDA.reasoning: Branch B fires (Buy the Dip in Trend). Branch A does NOT fire: daily Z-score is 1.06 (well within -2 to +2 range), RSI-14 at 59.74 is not extreme, and Bollinger %B at 0.57 places price near the middle band — no statistical blow-off. The 38.2% zone also coincides with the prior pivot high at $197.62 (5 tests) providing S/R confluence. Hourly OBV is diverging upward from falling price — a bullish divergence signal suggesting accumulation. Hourly Bollinger %B at 0.17 places price near the lower band, consistent with an oversold bounce setup. The lessons table shows 0% win rate on EMA pullback Fib dip-buy in the last 30 days (1 trade, 0 wins). This is a SECOND attempt at the same setup structure, which lowers conviction. However, the current entry level is meaningfully lower (~$10 below the prior entry), now testing the DAILY 38.2% Fib rather than the hourly level, representing a stronger support confluence. Key caution: daily volume on the pullback is elevated (1.52x average), which is a warning sign for Branch B (healthy pullbacks ideally have declining volume). Hourly MACD histogram remains negative, momentum not yet reversed. A reversal candle confirmation (hammer, engulfing) is required before entry per strategy rules — do not chase. Entry set slightly above the 38.2% Fib ($196.75) to allow for wick through the level, stop below the 50% Fib ($190.55), target at retest of recent swing high ($216.83).
